name: FlexRay CAPL Functions
title: FlexRay CAPL Functions
description: A detailed description of the ISO 10681-2 TP for FlexRay and the implemented CAPL functions can be found in the online help FlexRay TP ISO.
Checks are used to monitor the sending delay of messages which allow both cyclic and spontaneous transmission.
This check reports a problem, if the node has not send any of its Tx messages within a given time-interval.
This check allows the observation of the end of the activity of nodes.
This check is useful to supervise the value of signals.
FlexRay | Exemplary Usage of FlexRay Functions in CAPL
return: The callback occurs when the FlexRay Communication
tables: FLEXRAY Only available with option .FlexRay. In option .FlexRay several APIs are provided for receiving and transmitting FlexRay frames.
Callbacks Event Procedures FlexRay ISO TP General Functions
Object Test Feature Set for FlexRay Test Service Library for FlexRay
The callback occurs when the FlexRay Communication Controller (CC) has synchronized with the bus or the synchronization fails.
Is called in the event of a general error being detected on the FlexRay bus.

Is called up after a frame has been received in the specified slot and cycle. Note It is recommended to use on FRFrame only for the reception of valid data frames. To receive Null Frames and erroneous frames, please use the on frNullFrame or on frFrameError event procedures, since these events will not be received anymore with on frFrame from version 6.1.

Note It is recommended to use on FRFrame only for the reception of valid data frames. To receive Null Frames and erroneous frames, please use the on frNullFrame or on frFrameError event procedures, since these events will not be received anymore with on frFrame from version 6.1.

Note It is recommended to use on FRFrame only for the reception of valid data frames. To receive Null Frames and erroneous frames, please use the on frNullFrame or on frFrameError event procedures, since these events will not be received anymore with on frFrame from version 6.1.
Is called if a erroneous frame is received in the specified slot and cycle.
Is called if a Null Frame is received in the specified slot and cycle.
Is called when a PDU with the corresponding name is received.
Is called whenever there is a change of state on the FlexRay Communication Controller's protocol operation state machine.
Is called up in each cycle after the Slot is past.
Can be generated by FlexRay at the beginning of each communication cycle and contains the NM vector valid for this cycle.
Is called whenever a symbol (wake-up, CAS, MTS) is received.
Enable/disable all TX frames/PDUs of the specified node.
Copies the FlexRay protocol parameters to a FlexRay parameter object.
Returns the CRC of a received FlexRay frame.
Controls the routing for the dynamic frame part.
Controls the routing for static frames.
Updates the FlexRay Communication Controller’s (CC) send buffer with the current data from the send object.
Generates a FlexRay frame and sends it to the hardware.
Sends an MTS symbol in the next possible symbol window if the Communication Controller is in normal mode (synchronized).
Part of the payload of a frame will be incremented automatically on each transmission.
Writes the FlexRay protocol parameters from the FlexRay parameter object to the FlexRay interface’s Communication Controller.
Applies the external rate and offset correction values from the FlexRay parameters for the next cycle.
This function configures one of two possible key slots that are to be sent for a FlexRay bus.
Sets the payload (data) length of the object in bytes.
Puts the FlexRay Communication Controller into the desired Protocol Operation Mode.
Configures the hardware for sending the specified message.
All frames in the group are sent in the current cycle, or all frames are not sent until the next possible cycle.
Configures the hardware to transmit the specified PDU.
Activates the Trigger Output of the selected VN interface.
Updates the PDU payload in the assigned FlexRay frames.
Updates the FlexRay Communication Controller’s (CC) send buffer with the current data from the send object.
Returns the receive time of the frame or slot in nanoseconds.
Outputs the object from the program block of the analysis branch.
Initializes the FlexRay Communication Controller (CC) and begins a new start-up phase for the cluster or a new integrations phase in the cluster.
Initializes the FlexRay Communication Controller (CC) and generates the specified wake-up pattern before reintegration in the cluster or the start-up.
Waits for the occurrence of the valid specified FlexRay frame.
If a valid FlexRay frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the frame’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay frame.
Waits for the occurrence of the specified FlexRay Null Frame.
If a FlexRay Null Frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the frame’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay Null Frame.
Waiting for FlexRay Start Cycle Event
Waits for the occurrence of the specified FlexRay start cycle event.
If a FlexRay start cycle is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the event’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay start cycle event.
Waits for the occurrence of the specified FlexRay PDU event.
If a valid FlexRay PDU is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the PDU’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay PDU.
Waiting for FlexRay POC State Event
Waits for the occurrence of change of state on the FlexRay Communication Controller's protocol operation state machine.

If an event indicating a change of state on the FlexRay Communication Controller's protocol operation state machine is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the event’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the change of state event on the FlexRay Communication Controller's protocol operation state machine.
Waits for the occurrence of FlexRay erroneous frame event (frame error).
If a FlexRay erroneous frame is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the event’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay erroneous frame event.
Waits for the occurrence of a FlexRay symbol on the bus.
If a FlexRay symbol event is the last event that triggers a wait instruction, the event’s content can be called up.
Completes the current set of "joined events" with the FlexRay symbol event.
The check condition is violated if the payload length of the message does not agree with the specified payload length of the database (useful for dynamic frames).
Checks the occurrences of cyclic messages.
Checks the occurrences of cyclic messages.
Checks are used to monitor the sending delay of messages which allow both cyclic and spontaneous transmission.
This check is useful for spontaneous messages where one message is a result of another message; e.g. the check is used to check the processing time of the reference message.
Checks the absence of erroneous frames for the specified frame/slot on the bus.
Checks the absence of Null Frames for the specified frame/slot on the bus.
Check is used to monitor the minimum and/or maximum number for each of the defined messages within a specified cyclic time interval.
This check listens to the bus and reports a violation, if a message was received that is not defined in any of the databases that are associated to the current bus.
This check reports a problem, if the node has not send any of its Tx messages within a given time-interval.
This check allows the observation of the end of the activity of nodes.
The check is useful to observe the constancy of a signal value.
Checks the absence of the specified message on the bus.
This checks creates an error event if particular time is expired.
This check is useful to supervise the value of signals.
